findings.title: Cosine similarity definition missing ‖z‖ in denominator
findings.snippet: DEFINITION 1: The cosine similarity of two nonzero vectors $\boldsymbol{y}$ and $\boldsymbol{z}$ is $\rho(\boldsymbol{y}, \boldsymbol{z})=\frac{\boldsymbol{y} \cdot \boldsymbol{z}}{\|\boldsymbol{y}\|}$.
findings.explanation: The standard cosine similarity is y·z / (‖y‖·‖z‖). The denominator is missing ‖z‖. This definition is used pervasively in Theorem 1, Corollary 1, Propositions 1–2, and their proofs. The proofs sometimes rely on ‖u^ℓ‖=1 to cancel the missing term, but the definition itself is mathematically incorrect as stated.

findings.title: Circular self-referencing variable in Example 2 change of variables
findings.snippet: Performing the change of variables $b_{i}=\left[\tau-b_{i}\right] / 2$ and $\beta=-\tilde{\beta} / 2$
findings.explanation: The left-hand side defines b_i but the right-hand side also uses b_i, making it circular and undefined. Based on context (the tilde notation for status-quo public-goods parameters), this should read b_i = [τ − \tilde{b}_i] / 2, using the tilde'd base-level parameter defined earlier in Example 2.

findings.title: Minimizers incorrectly called 'maximizers' for λ_n and λ_{n-1}
findings.snippet: Moreover, the eigenvector $\boldsymbol{u}^{n}$ is a maximizer of the first problem, while $\boldsymbol{u}^{n-1}$ is a maximizer of the second
findings.explanation: The two problems immediately above are minimization problems (min_{u:‖u‖=1} Σ g_{ij} u_i u_j). Therefore u^n and u^{n-1} are minimizers, not maximizers. Compare with the analogous paragraph for λ_1, λ_2 (line ~316), which correctly calls them maximizers of max problems.

findings.title: Assumption 3 missing squared norm: C < ‖b̂‖ instead of C < ‖b̂‖²
findings.explanation: The cost function K(b, b̂) = Σ(b_i − b̂_i)² has units of ‖·‖². The preceding paragraph correctly states the bliss-point condition as C ≥ ‖b̂‖². Assumption 3 should mirror this with C < ‖b̂‖² (not C < ‖b̂‖). Footnote 34 also uses Σ b̂_ℓ² ≤ C, confirming the squared version is intended.

findings.title: Lagrangian missing exponent: b̂_ℓ instead of b̂_ℓ²
findings.explanation: The Lagrangian is derived from the objective max_x w Σ α_ℓ (1+x_ℓ)² b̂_ℓ² (displayed a few lines above at (IT-PC after change of variable)). The first summand should therefore contain b̂_ℓ² (squared), not b̂_ℓ. Without the square, the subsequent first-order condition derivation would be incorrect.
findings.fix: Change $\underline{\hat{b}}_{\ell}$ to $\underline{\hat{b}}_{\ell}^{2}$ in the first summation.

findings.title: Denominator sign flipped in discussion of bottom gap under strategic substitutes
findings.snippet: If $\beta<0$, then the term $\alpha_{n-1} /(\alpha_{n-1}-\alpha_{n})$ is large when the difference $\lambda_{n-1}-\lambda_{n}$... is small.
findings.explanation: Under strategic substitutes (β<0), α_n > α_{n-1}, so α_{n-1} − α_n < 0. Proposition 2 part 2 correctly writes the ratio as α_{n-1}/(α_n − α_{n-1}), which is positive. The text here reverses the denominator to (α_{n-1} − α_n), flipping the sign.

findings.title: Δb* used in proof of Proposition 2 without definition
findings.explanation: The symbol Δb* is never formally defined. All preceding text and Theorem 1 use y* = b* − b̂ for the optimal intervention vector. The notation Δb* appears only in the proof of Proposition 2 (lines ~569–596), creating an undefined-symbol issue and inconsistency with y*.

findings.title: Underline missing on b̂_ℓ in Theorem 1's equation (6)
findings.explanation: This equation sums over principal-component indices ℓ. The notation z̲ = U⊤z was defined for projections onto the PC basis, so the correct symbol here is b̲̂_ℓ (underlined). The same inconsistency recurs at lines ~233, 471, 483, 491, and multiple places in the Proposition 2 proof. In some places the underline is present (e.g., the reformulation at line ~227), in others it is absent, creating a systematic drift.
findings.fix: Replace $\hat{b}_{\ell}$ with $\underline{\hat{b}}_{\ell}$ in all equations indexed by principal component ℓ (equations (6), (8), the marginal-return/cost display, the FOC, the budget-pinning equation, and the proof of Proposition 2).

findings.title: β bolded as vector in two places
findings.explanation: β is a scalar parameter throughout the paper and is never bolded in its definition or in any other usage. At line ~175 ('the ℓth diagonal entry of [I − βΛ]⁻¹') and line ~436 in the proof of Theorem 1 ('a* = [I − βΛ]⁻¹ b̲'), β appears in boldface (\boldsymbol{\beta}), suggesting it is a vector or matrix. This contradicts its scalar nature.

findings.title: Eigenvector entry subscripts swapped: u_ℓ^i vs u_i^ℓ
findings.explanation: The paper consistently uses u_i^ℓ for the ith entry of the ℓth eigenvector (e.g., equation for a_i* at line ~186: u_i^ℓ b̲_ℓ). In the proof of Proposition 2, the subscript and superscript are reversed to u_ℓ^i. This swaps the meaning: u_ℓ^i would denote the ℓth entry of the ith eigenvector.

findings.title: Spurious α₂* in proof of Proposition 2 welfare bound
findings.explanation: The symbol α₂* is undefined. There is no asterisked version of the amplification factors α_ℓ anywhere in the paper. Context (comparing with the derived bound on the next line and with line ~557 which yields (2α₁ − α₂)‖b̂‖²) confirms this should simply be α₂, with the asterisk being a stray character.

findings.title: Equation (2) drops bold formatting for vectors and matrices
findings.explanation: Equation (2) uses plain (non-bold) I, G, a*, b. But the immediately preceding text and equation (3) right after both use bold: 𝐈, 𝐆, 𝐚*, 𝐛. The convention of bold for vectors/matrices is established at line ~39.

findings.title: Set name inconsistency: N vs 𝒩
findings.snippet: we assume that for every $i \in N, g_{ii}=0$
findings.explanation: The set of individuals is defined as 𝒩 = {1,…,n} using calligraphic font. One occurrence uses plain N instead of 𝒩. All other uses in the paper correctly write 𝒩.

findings.title: Prime placement: α_ℓ' vs α_{ℓ'} in Proposition 1 proof
findings.explanation: In the denominator fraction, α_ℓ' places the prime on α (suggesting a different quantity), whereas the intended symbol is α_{ℓ'} — the amplification factor for the ℓ'-th component — matching the numerator's α_{ℓ'}.

findings.title: Missing summation index i in cost function (10)
findings.explanation: The summation is written as Σ_{∈𝒩} with no index variable, whereas it should be Σ_{i∈𝒩}.

findings.title: Malformed summation in proof's reformulated problem
findings.explanation: The summation appears as 'Σ ℓ=1^n' with the lower bound detached from the sigma, rather than the proper Σ_{ℓ=1}^{n}. This is likely a LaTeX rendering/conversion artifact.

findings.title: Awkward transpose placement: (a̲⊤)*  instead of (a̲*)⊤
findings.explanation: The notation (a̲⊤)* places the equilibrium asterisk outside the transpose, which is non-standard and ambiguous. The intended expression is (a̲*)⊤ a̲* — transpose of the equilibrium action vector in the PC basis, dotted with itself.
